title: Platform: doc.ingest system event not auto-fired on ingest confirm
description:
## Problem
When a source is confirmed via POST /sources/:id/confirm, the Eve platform should automatically fire the doc.ingest system event to trigger the ingestion-pipeline workflow. It doesn't.

Additionally, manually running the workflow via `eve workflow run ingestion-pipeline` doesn't materialize the uploaded document resources into the agent workspace (.eve/resources/index.json is missing), so the extraction agent has nothing to read.

## Evidence (E2E test Mar 25)
- Source created and confirmed: id=77d32d19, eve_ingest_id=ing_01kmj0crz9espbe6srwp0kg686
- Source status changed to `processing` but no pipeline jobs appeared after 10+ minutes
- Manual event emit via `eve event emit --project proj_01kkh30080e00rw62jqhkchwbk --type doc.ingest --source system` created event evt_01kmj14mmhespbe6tbv33b0m4g with status=completed but no workflow triggered
- Manual `eve workflow run ingestion-pipeline` created job eden-72c589c0 but extraction agent found no document (.eve/resources/index.json missing)
- Extraction agent logs: "The .eve/resources/index.json file does not exist — no document has been materialized into the workspace"

## Two distinct issues
1. Platform ingest confirm doesn't auto-fire doc.ingest event to trigger workflow
2. Manual workflow run doesn't materialize ingest resources into workspace

## Impact
Blocks Phase 2 (document ingestion) and Phase 7 (second document ingestion) of the E2E test plan.

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Phase 2, steps 2.1-2.3)
- Workflow config: eve/workflows.yaml (ingestion-pipeline trigger: system: event: doc.ingest)
- Eve ingest service: apps/api/src/sources/eve-ingest.service.ts
- Source confirm: apps/api/src/sources/sources.service.ts:136
close_reason: Root cause: three platform bugs fixed in eve-horizon-2. (1) Manifest sync did two POSTs creating separate records - now merged into single POST. (2) CLI eve event emit didnt auto-prefix system event types - now auto-prefixes. (3) eve workflow run didnt pass --input to invoke - now wraps in body.input correctly.

title: [bug] doc.ingest event → ingestion-pipeline workflow not triggering
description: The platform's ingest flow works correctly — events emitted via IngestService.confirm() with type 'system.doc.ingest' DO trigger the ingestion-pipeline workflow (confirmed by job eden-b8586325 completing successfully). The E2E test failure was caused by using 'eve event emit --type doc.ingest' which creates events with type 'doc.ingest' (missing 'system.' prefix). The trigger matcher expects fully-qualified types ('system.doc.ingest'). The web UI upload flow (create → upload → confirm → workflow) works end-to-end. Sources reach 'done' status when the pipeline completes.
close_reason: Not a bug: platform works correctly. E2E test used wrong event type format via eve event emit (doc.ingest vs system.doc.ingest). The web UI upload flow works end-to-end.

title: Chat map editing agent mutates map directly instead of creating changeset
description:
The coordinator agent still calls direct entity creation endpoints (POST /tasks, POST /personas) even after removing those endpoints from the skill documentation. The agent has bash/fetch tools and discovers endpoints on its own. Skill-level instructions alone cannot prevent this.

The fix requires SERVER-SIDE enforcement: the Eden API should reject direct entity creation requests from agent actors (identified by the service token or agent-specific auth). When an agent calls POST /tasks directly, the API should return 403 with a message directing the agent to use POST /changesets instead.

Alternative: Add middleware that detects agent auth tokens and redirects direct entity mutations to auto-create a changeset wrapper.
close_reason: Fixed: added ChangesetOnlyGuard that returns 403 when agent (job_token) calls direct entity mutation endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE on tasks, personas, activities, steps). Agents must use POST /changesets.

title: Source status not updating after ingestion pipeline completes
description: After the 3-step ingestion pipeline (ingest → extract → synthesize) completes successfully, the source record status remains 'processing' instead of updating to 'synthesized'. Root cause: callback_url is null in the Eve ingest record because EDEN_API_URL env var is not set in the Eden sandbox deployment, so EveIngestService.createIngest() never includes a callback URL. Fix: Set EDEN_API_URL in Eden sandbox environment variables.
close_reason: Fixed: refreshed EVE_SERVICE_TOKEN (was 401 unsupported algorithm), corrected EDEN_API_URL to public URL. Source creation returns 201, pipeline completes, callback updates status to done.

title: Platform: chat routes should support with_apis for agent→app API access
description: The workflows.yaml with_apis config grants API access to workflow-triggered agents, but chat-routed agents (via chat.yaml) don't get the same treatment. The coordinator agent running from a chat message has no way to call the deployed app's API endpoints. **Why:** Chat is the primary agent interaction model but agents can't act on the app's data. **How to apply:** Add with_apis support to chat.yaml routes, or make it automatic for team dispatch.
close_reason: Worked around by teaching agents to call Eden API directly via node fetch from harness sandbox. Skills now include API URLs, auth token discovery, and endpoint reference. The with_apis route feature is still a platform enhancement worth tracking separately.

title: Agent API auth: job tokens rejected by Eden AuthGuard
description: Chat-routed agents get an Eve job JWT (type:job, sub:system) but Eden's AuthGuard expects Eve user tokens (type:user, sub:user_xxx). The agent can see the API on port 4812 inside the cluster but gets 401 when calling /projects. Need to either: (a) make AuthGuard accept job tokens by extracting org_id/user_id from the job JWT claims, or (b) have Eve inject a service token that the agent can use. **Why:** This blocks ALL agent→API interactions (map editing, changeset creation, question management). Scenario 10 cannot pass. **How to apply:** Update AuthGuard to accept job-scoped JWTs, mapping job claims to DbContext.
close_reason: Agent auth works - job tokens accepted via verifyEveToken middleware, agents read credentials.json for Eden API auth

title: Expert panel review not persisted as review record
description:
## Problem
When the PM coordinator dispatches an expert panel review, the comprehensive multi-expert analysis is delivered only as chat messages. No formal review record is created in the reviews table, so the Reviews page shows 0 reviews.

## Evidence (E2E test Mar 25)
- Chat thread thr_01kkhdr9wke4ft6ar86x67xqwx: 100 messages including full 7-expert synthesis
- eden review list --project \$PROJECT_ID: 0 reviews
- Coordinator job eden-aaeb2b78 completed (done), 7 expert sub-jobs cancelled (solo path)
- The agent produced an excellent synthesis with Tech Lead, UX, Business Analyst, GTM, Risk, QA, and Devil's Advocate perspectives — but only in chat

## Why this matters
1. Reviews page is empty despite expert panel actually running
2. Review data is trapped in chat messages, not queryable as structured review records
3. expert_opinions table not populated — can't programmatically access individual expert perspectives
4. The review synthesis/status metadata (complete, expert count) is not tracked

## Expected behavior
The coordinator should call the Eden API to create a review record (POST /projects/:id/reviews) with:
- synthesis text (the combined analysis)
- per-expert opinions
- status: complete
- reference to the triggering chat thread

## Potential fixes
1. Update PM coordinator SKILL.md to call `eden review create` after synthesizing expert opinions
2. Add review creation to the coordinator's post-synthesis workflow
3. Consider auto-creating a review record when the coordinator detects a panel review request

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Phase 4.3)
- PM coordinator skill: skills/pm/SKILL.md
- Review service: apps/api/src/reviews/
- Chat thread: thr_01kkhdr9wke4ft6ar86x67xqwx (incept5 org, sandbox env)
close_reason: Skill instructions updated: solo-path review creation now uses CRITICAL/MUST language with explicit code block. Both skills/coordinator/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/pm-coordinator/SKILL.md synced. No platform gap — agent instruction clarity was the root cause.

title: Agents self-accept changesets — bypasses human review flow
description:
## Problem
All agents (map-generator, map-chat/coordinator) create changesets and immediately accept them, bypassing the human review workflow. The changeset model (create draft → human reviews → accept/reject) is a core Eden feature, but agents never leave changesets in draft status.

## Evidence (E2E test Mar 25)
- Wizard agent: created changeset "Initial story map for CloudMetrics" and self-accepted (changeset_id=null in status response, changeset found with status=accepted)
- Map-chat (complex): created changeset "Expand Customer Health Scoring..." and self-accepted (no draft changeset visible)
- Map-chat (simple): created changeset "Add Temporary Test Activity" and self-accepted
- Result: 0 draft changesets available for human review at any point during the test

## Why this matters
1. Users can't preview or modify AI-proposed changes before they hit the map
2. Per-item accept/reject (a key Eden feature) is never exercised
3. Rejection testing (Phase 9.1) is impossible because no draft changesets exist
4. Audit trail shows changes but users had no opportunity to intervene

## Potential fixes
1. Add a `auto_accept: false` flag to agent SKILL.md instructions — agents create drafts, don't accept
2. Add a project-level setting: `agent_changeset_mode: draft|auto_accept`
3. Modify the map-generator and map-chat skills to always create drafts and notify the user

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Phase 1.5 self-acceptance detection, Phase 9.1 rejection test blocked)
- Map generator skill: skills/map-generator/SKILL.md
- Map chat skill: skills/map-chat/SKILL.md
- Changeset service: apps/api/src/changesets/changesets.service.ts
close_reason: Two-layer fix: (1) All 5 changeset-creating skills now have NEVER accept rule. (2) API-level guard rejects accept calls for agent-created changesets (source != null/manual/web). No platform gap — separation of duties is domain-specific.

title: Platform: app events (changeset.accepted, question.answered) not auto-triggering workflows
description:
## Problem
Eden emits app events via EveEventsService (POST /projects/{id}/events with source: 'app') when changesets are accepted and questions are answered. These events should trigger the alignment-check and question-evolution workflows respectively, but the platform doesn't dispatch them.

## Evidence (E2E test Mar 25)
- Wizard agent self-accepted changeset at 08:03 UTC — no alignment-check workflow triggered
- Question da5d8119 evolved/answered at ~09:00 UTC — no question-evolution workflow triggered
- Both workflows work when manually invoked via `eve workflow run`:
  - `eve workflow run question-evolution` → job eden-aec505ff completed in 164s
  - Alignment questions (18) were created by the wizard agent directly, not by an alignment workflow

## How events are emitted
- EveEventsService.emit() in apps/api/src/common/eve-events.service.ts
- POST to {EVE_API_URL}/projects/{EVE_PROJECT_ID}/events with body: {type: "changeset.accepted", source: "app", payload_json: {...}}
- Requires EVE_API_URL, EVE_PROJECT_ID, and EVE_SERVICE_TOKEN env vars to be set in the deployed API

## Possible causes
1. EVE_API_URL or EVE_SERVICE_TOKEN not configured as secrets in the sandbox environment
2. Platform event router not matching app-sourced events to app: trigger workflows
3. Event emitted but silently fails (the service catches errors and only logs warnings)

## Workflow triggers (eve/workflows.yaml)
- alignment-check: trigger: app: event: changeset.accepted
- question-evolution: trigger: app: event: question.answered

## Impact
Blocks automated alignment scanning after changeset acceptance and automated question evolution after answers. Both workflows must be manually triggered via `eve workflow run`.

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Phase 3, Phase 6)
- Event service: apps/api/src/common/eve-events.service.ts
- Changeset emit: apps/api/src/changesets/changesets.service.ts:283
- Question emit: apps/api/src/questions/questions.service.ts:345
- Workflow config: eve/workflows.yaml
close_reason: Root cause: EVE_API_URL, EVE_PROJECT_ID, EVE_SERVICE_TOKEN secrets were all empty on staging. Set all three, added startup warning in EveEventsService, redeployed. Verified end-to-end: test event emitted → orchestrator matched question-evolution workflow (1 of 4 evaluated) → job eden-60c651db created.

title: [bug] generate-map/status endpoint stuck on running after job completes
description: The GET /projects/:id/generate-map/status endpoint returns status='running' indefinitely even after the Eve job reaches phase=done. The poll loop exhausts without ever seeing 'complete'. Root cause: the API likely checks a DB field that the agent never updates (the agent uses changeset self-acceptance and direct mutations rather than updating the job status endpoint).
close_reason: Fixed: status endpoint now reads job.phase (not job.status) from Eve API. Returns 'complete' when job phase is 'done'.

title: Question-evolution workflow timeout too short (300s vs 327s actual)

The orchestrator cancelled the job at 300s, yet the harness continued and completed successfully 27 seconds later — creating the changeset but leaving the job in cancelled state. This is a race condition. Fix: Increase question-evolution workflow timeout to 600s to provide adequate margin.
close_reason: Timeout already increased to 600s in workflows.yaml. Verified: question-evolution job completed in 102s, well within limit. No timeout cancellation.

title: Alignment agent storm prevention not deduplicating questions effectively

The agent checks existing questions but still creates overlapping ones from slightly different angles (e.g., Q-20 about persona assignments overlaps Q-12, Q-6, Q-7). After a trivial changeset acceptance, 4 new questions were created with meaningful overlap to existing ones. Fix: Implement semantic deduplication at the question-creation level, or add a cooldown/debounce on alignment triggers to prevent question storms.
close_reason: Strengthened alignment skill dedup instructions. Verified: first alignment run explicitly skipped 6+ overlapping questions, created only 2 genuinely new ones. Storm prevention confirmed (second run created 0 new questions).

title: Agent job execution times exceed targets by 2-3x
description:
## Problem
Most agent jobs run significantly slower than the targets defined in the E2E test plan. This increases user wait times and platform costs.

## Timing data (E2E test Mar 25)

| Agent Job | Actual | Target | Overshoot |
|-----------|--------|--------|-----------|
| Map Generator (wizard) | 355s (5.9min) | <120s (2min) | 3.0x |
| Expert Panel (coordinator) | ~480s (8min) | <360s (6min) | 1.3x |
| Map-Chat (simple/no-op) | 164s (2.7min) | <90s (1.5min) | 1.8x |
| Map-Chat (complex) | 306s (5.1min) | <180s (3min) | 1.7x |
| Map-Chat (question add) | 31s | <90s | OK |
| Question Evolution | 164s (2.7min) | <120s (2min) | 1.4x |

## Notes
- The "simple" map-chat job (164s) was actually a no-op (persona already existed) but still took 2.7 minutes — the agent spent time reading the map and reasoning before concluding no action was needed
- The coordinator went solo path (cancelled 7 experts) but still took 8 minutes to synthesize
- Only the question-addition chat (31s) met its target comfortably

## Potential optimizations
1. Agent skills could be more directive about early-exit when no changes needed
2. Map reads could be cached or scoped (read only relevant activities, not full map)
3. Coordinator could parallelize expert dispatch more efficiently
4. Wizard could use structured output to reduce tool-call overhead

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Agent Efficiency Protocol, Phase 10.2)
- Agent harness config: eve/x-eve.yaml
- Agent skills: skills/*/SKILL.md
close_reason: Added fast harness profile (sonnet/low reasoning). Switched map-generator (3x over target) and map-chat (1.8x) to fast profile. Added early-exit and targeted-lookup instructions to map-chat skill. Platform gap: no soft time budget for agents — agents don't know they should be fast. Not worth platform engineering now.

title: Changeset source field null when created via map-chat agent
description:
## Problem
When the map-chat agent (or coordinator routing to map-chat) creates a changeset, the source field is null instead of being set to "map-chat". This makes it impossible to filter changesets by source to identify which agent produced them.

## Evidence (E2E test Mar 25)
- Changeset "Expand Customer Health Scoring..." (098087df): source=null, created via map-chat
- Changeset "Add Temporary Test Activity" (6fa9e3a5): source=null, created via map-chat  
- Changeset "Initial story map..." (5dd564bf): source="manual", created via wizard

The test plan step 2.5 tries to find ingestion changesets with:
  jq '[.[] | select(.source == "ingestion" or .source == "synthesis")]'
This would also fail for map-chat changesets since source is null.

## Expected behavior
Changesets should have a source field matching the agent that created them: "map-generator", "map-chat", "synthesis", "question-evolution", etc.

## Likely cause
The Eden CLI `eden changeset create` command or the changeset API doesn't set the source field, and agent skills don't pass it explicitly.

## References
- Test plan: docs/plans/e2e-agent-test-plan.md (Phase 5)
- Changeset create endpoint: apps/api/src/changesets/changesets.service.ts
- Eden CLI changeset commands: cli/
close_reason: API now auto-derives source and actor from agent identity (email {slug}@eve.agent) when not provided in the changeset JSON. No more null source for agent-created changesets. Minor platform gap: EveUser interface should surface agentSlug — filed mentally for next SDK release.

title: BUG: Chat panel reuses stale thread instead of starting fresh
description: When opening chat panel, no thread is selected (activeThread=null) which is correct. But after sending the first message, the thread created becomes sticky. On 'New thread' click, activeThread resets. However the real issue is that thread list is loaded but never rendered — users can't switch between threads. **Why:** Thread switching UI not implemented — only one conversation per session. **How to apply:** Either show thread list or always create a new thread per session.
close_reason: Deferred — P3 UX enhancement. Chat works for single-conversation flows which covers all agent scenarios. Thread list/switching is a polish item for Phase 4.
